text.title: django-service-layer Output Contract
text.summary: Output is Python source defining service functions. The validator parses each function and checks the schema below.

example.code:
from django.db import transaction from apps.orders.models import Order, OrderItem from core.exceptions import ValidationError
def order_create(*, user, items: list[dict]) -> Order: if not items: raise ValidationError("at least one item required")
with transaction.atomic(): order = Order(user=user, total=sum(i["price"] for i in items)) order.full_clean() order.save() OrderItem.objects.bulk_create([OrderItem(order=order, **i) for i in items])
transaction.on_commit(lambda: notify_user.delay(order_id=order.id))
return order
example.note: Valid: kwarg-only signature, atomic boundary in service, full_clean before save, domain ValidationError, on_commit-wrapped Celery dispatch.

example.code:
from rest_framework.exceptions import ValidationError as DRFValidationError
def order_create(user, items): # positional args if not items: raise DRFValidationError("empty") # HTTP exception in service order = Order.objects.create( # no full_clean user=user, total=sum(i["price"] for i in items), ) for i in items: OrderItem.objects.create(order=order, **i) notify_user.delay(order_id=order.id) # outside on_commit, no atomic return order
example.note: Invalid: positional args, DRF exception (HTTP coupling), `objects.create` skips clean(), no atomic boundary, .delay() unguarded.
